prediction.rationale: ₹27cr with zero IPL data across 2023, 2024 and 2025. World-class when fit — but there is genuinely no recent form to anchor a score. Pure reputation play at a record price.
Record overseas price (₹25.2cr) for a player returning from back surgery with no IPL 2024 or 2025 data. KKR paid a massive trust premium on potential, not proof.
639 runs in 2023, 741 in 2024, 657 in 2025 — 600+ in three consecutive seasons is a near-unique track record. At ₹21cr the price is meaningful but the floor is exceptionally well-documented.
527 runs in 2024, Orange Cap (759 runs) in 2025. Clear upward trajectory. At ₹8.5cr he is almost certainly the best value batter in the 2026 auction. GT retained him at a fraction of market rate.
Huge improvement from 2024 (346 runs) to 2025 (717 runs at 175 SR). Two-season average is strong and the 2025 trajectory suggests the floor is rising.
20 wickets in 2023, consistent 15 in both 2024 and 2025, economy crept from 6.48 to 7.10. At ₹18cr the three-season average puts him in fair value territory — reliable but not underpriced.
15 wkts in 2023, 12 in 2024, Purple Cap (25 wkts) in 2025. Three-season average of 17.3 wickets at ₹9.5cr is excellent value. GT retained him wisely.
530 in 2023, dipped to 351 in 2024, bounced to 603 in 2025. Inconsistent across three seasons. Three-season average is decent but not ₹26.75cr decent.
Three seasons of elite dual contribution at just ₹6cr. Slight dip in 2025 but the three-season average is still exceptional. Among the clearest steals in the 2026 auction.
Declining output across all three seasons — fewer runs, fewer wickets, rising economy. New team (CSK to RR) adds uncertainty. ₹18cr demands more than any of the three seasons delivered.
Consistent 18–19 wickets across both seasons but economy drifting upward (8.60 → 8.85). At ₹18cr with KKR, two-season average supports fair value — not a steal, not overpaid.
16 wkts in 2023, 14 in 2024, 16 in 2025 — steady consistency with improving economy. At ₹7.2cr the three-season average consistently outperforms the price. RR bought him well.
9 wkts in 2023, 11 in 2024, 24 in 2025 — dramatic step-up with improving economy. Three-season average of 14.7 wickets at ₹10cr is sharp. The upward trajectory strengthens the case further.
Only 2025 data (8 matches, 162 SR) — one season is a limited sample, widening the confidence band. But ₹3cr is so low that even discounted output comfortably beats the price.
Absent from both IPL 2024 and 2025. Was exceptional in 2022–23 but that data is stale. SRH are betting on a return to form at ₹11.25cr with no recent evidence to lean on.
Record-equalling uncapped price (₹14.2cr) with zero IPL history. Widest confidence band in the dataset. CSK's development record is excellent but no IPL data means no score anchor.
Same record uncapped price as Prashant Veer. CSK made a bold double bet on domestic talent. Zero IPL data makes this a wide-range prediction entirely.
No IPL history but strong international form for SA. ₹7cr is a moderate bet. MI have a good eye for overseas openers. Early signs are encouraging.
328 runs at 156 SR in 2025 with modest bowling contribution. At ₹7cr (down from ₹23.75cr with KKR) this is a sharp buy for RCB if he settles into a clear role.
531 in 2023, 531 in 2024, 442 in 2025 — remarkably consistent then dipped. At ₹18cr with CSK in a new environment, the three-season average puts this at fair value.
369 runs at 196 SR in 2023, 567 at 191 in 2024, 478 at 188 in 2025. The most consistent elite SR batter across three seasons. At ₹14cr this is exceptional value.
Steady dual contributor across three seasons. Economy improving (7.90 → 8.22 → 8.05). At ₹16.5cr the price is high but consistent all-round output justifies it — squarely fair value.
21 wkts in 2023, 19 in 2024, 15 in 2025 — slight decline but excellent economy all three years (sub-8.2). At ₹13.25cr the wrist-spin quality and economy make this fair to slightly good value.
13 then 15 wickets at ₹4cr across two seasons. Economy is a touch high but the wicket-taking rate is strong. Excellent price-to-output ratio makes this a reliable steal.
418 at 176 SR in 2023, 479 at 180 in 2024, 487 at 172 in 2025 — remarkably consistent. The issue is the price: ₹23cr is a very steep ask even for this output level.
219 runs in 2023 then exploded to 484 at 204 SR in 2024, 392 at 186 in 2025. Three seasons of elite or near-elite strike rate. Minimal bowling but batting quality at ₹14cr is strong value.
22 wickets in 2025 at excellent economy (8.05) — strong debut IPL season. Only one season available so confidence band is wider, but the output-to-price ratio at ₹12.5cr looks very good.
Consistent dual contributor across three seasons — batting improving, bowling economy edging down. At ₹8.75cr retained by PBKS, the three-season average strongly outperforms the price.
18 wkts in 2023, 21 in 2024, 16 in 2025 — slight dip but economy consistently tight. At ₹12cr the three-season average justifies the price — strong but not a bargain given the step-down in 2025.
343 in 2023, 343 in 2024, 468 in 2025 — clear upward trend in both volume and strike rate. At ₹8cr this is excellent value. MI have a proven match-winner at a very reasonable price.
532 in 2023, 417 in 2024, 390 in 2025 — declining volume and SR across three seasons. At ₹16.35cr MI pay a legacy premium. Three-season average is decent but the trajectory is the wrong direction.
625 in 2023, 435 in 2024, 559 in 2025 — high volume across all three seasons with consistently elite SR. At ₹18cr this is solid value for a top-order batter of this calibre.
520 in 2023, 520 in 2024, 539 in 2025 — remarkably consistent with improving SR (131 → 136 → 149). At ₹14cr this is quietly good value for a reliable anchor batter across three seasons.
890 runs in 2023 (Orange Cap), 426 in 2024, 650 in 2025 — three-season average of 655 runs. Even discounting the 2023 peak, the volume is elite. At ₹16.5cr he is underpriced.
16 wkts in 2023, 16 in 2024, dipped to 11 in 2025 with rising economy (9.38 → 9.50). At ₹18.5cr SRH are banking on captain leadership value beyond the stats. Hard to justify on numbers alone.
Good batter across three seasons but bowling contribution is thin and expensive. At ₹14cr (big jump from ₹3.8cr in 2024) he needs to develop his bowling to justify the price on our all-rounder model.

Consistently destructive batting SR (177→189→178) and solid wicket-taking across three seasons but economy is pricey and output slightly declining. At ₹12cr the three-season average lands in fair value.
418 in 2023, 329 in 2024, 287 in 2025 — declining but consistently 150+ SR across three seasons. Bowling is token. At ₹4cr the batting contribution still easily beats the price.
19 in 2023, 19 in 2024, 18 in 2025 — elite consistency. Economy improved in 2025 (8.98 → 8.60). At ₹18cr the price is high but three-season consistency supports a fair value rating.
14 in 2023, 16 in 2024, 13 in 2025 with rising economy across all three. Dipping output at a high price point. At ₹10.75cr the three-season average delivers borderline value.
Identical wicket tally (13) across both seasons but economy is drifting upward (9.35→9.55). At ₹10.75cr the two-season average barely justifies the price. Needs improvement in economy.
13 in 2023, 12 in 2024, 16 in 2025 with meaningfully improving economy (8.93→9.12→8.48). Clear upward trajectory in 2025. At ₹11.5cr retained by GT, the improvement makes this a good bet.
306 runs and 11 wkts in 2023, modest in 2024, slight improvement in 2025. Still short of elite all-rounder output for ₹15cr across three seasons. Fair value at best.

name: Prithvi Shaw
prior_note: Returning from limited game time in 2024-25

prediction.rationale: No recent IPL data but ₹3.2cr is a low-risk bet. Was electric in his early career (189+ SR). If he finds form, this could be a significant steal — but the form gap is a real concern.

Consistent dual contributor across three seasons — runs and wickets both improving, economy improving (7.92→8.45→7.85). At ₹5.75cr this is very good value. RCB retained their most reliable all-rounder cheaply.
18 wkts in 2023, 13 in 2024, 18 in 2025 — inconsistent output and economy worsened noticeably (8.18→9.05). At ₹18cr PBKS are paying top dollar for a spinner whose economy is a growing concern.
590 in 2023, 372 in 2024, 332 in 2025 — declining but three-season average of 431 runs at ₹6cr is outstanding value. Even the weakest season easily outperforms this price point.
17 wickets in his first full season at ₹2.6cr — strong debut output. Economy of 8.95 is a touch high but at this price any wicket-taking pace bowler is a steal. One season only, hence the wider band.

Useful dual contributor in 2025 at ₹2cr — solid SR batting and tidy bowling economy. At this price even modest output is a steal. One season of data widens the band but the price makes the risk trivial.

474 in 2023, 279 in 2024, 362 in 2025 — inconsistent across three seasons. At ₹13cr this is a steep ask for a finisher. Three-season average is solid but the price requires more top-order consistency.
